lesson.title: Square Roots & Cube Roots
lesson.body: The **square root** of n is the number r where r² = n. Written √n. Example: √25 = 5 because 5² = 25. Every positive number has two square roots: √25 = ±5, but by convention √ means the positive (principal) root. The **cube root** ∛n is the number r where r³ = n. Cube roots can be negative: ∛(−8) = −2 because (−2)³ = −8. Perfect squares: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81, 100. Perfect cubes: 1, 8, 27, 64, 125.

rooms.question: What is √144?
rooms.explanation: √144 = 12 because 12² = 144. To verify: 12 × 12 = 144 ✓. Memorizing perfect squares up to 15² = 225 is very useful for speed.

rooms.question: Which of the following is irrational?
rooms.explanation: √16=4, √25=5, √49=7 are all integers (rational). √20 ≈ 4.472..., a non-repeating decimal — irrational. A square root is irrational whenever the radicand is not a perfect square.

rooms.question: What is ∛27?
rooms.explanation: ∛27 = 3 because 3³ = 27. Unlike square roots, cube roots of negatives are real: ∛(−27) = −3.

rooms.question: Between which two consecutive integers does √50 lie?
rooms.explanation: 7² = 49 and 8² = 64. Since 49 < 50 < 64, we have 7 < √50 < 8. √50 ≈ 7.07. This estimation technique works for any irrational square root.

rooms.question: Match each radical to its value — order from smallest to largest result:
rooms.explanation: ∛8=2, √9=3, ∛64=4, √36=6. Note that ∛64=4 < √36=6, even though 64 > 36, because cube roots grow more slowly than square roots for the same radicand.

boss.name: The Root Wraith
boss.taunt: Rational or irrational — your fate is sealed.

questions.question: Simplify: √(−9)
questions.explanation: There is no real number r such that r² = −9 (any real number squared is non-negative). √(−9) is not a real number — it equals 3i in the complex number system, but at this level, the answer is 'not a real number'. This contrasts with ∛(−9) which IS real (≈ −2.08).

questions.question: A square room has an area of 75 m². What is the approximate side length to the nearest meter?
questions.explanation: Side = √75. Estimate: 8²=64, 9²=81. Since 64 < 75 < 81, we have 8 < √75 < 9. More precisely, √75 ≈ 8.66. To the nearest meter, 8.66 rounds to 9 m.

questions.question: Which equation has no real solution?
questions.explanation: x²=16 → x=±4 (real solutions). x²=−4 → no real solution (squares can't be negative). x³=−8 → x=−2 (real, cube root of negative). x³=27 → x=3 (real). Key distinction: even powers reject negatives on the right; odd powers accept them.
